# Preface {.unnumbered}

**Core Sample** is a series of standalone monographs on data science, each one *an inch
wide and a mile deep*. An article picks a single, tightly-scoped question and follows it
all the way to the bottom — every derivation shown, every step on the page, nothing above
high-school calculus assumed to be remembered.

## Who it's for

The reader is a coder: a computer-science background, now studying data science at the
graduate level, fluent in code and rigorous reasoning, but **rusty** on the mathematics.
You have seen this material before and can absolutely follow it again — provided nothing
is skipped. So nothing is. Definitions, identities, and the small algebraic moves you once
knew by heart are all written out. The goal of every article is graduate-level command of
its topic: enough to pass the course, answer the interview question, and know when the
technique is the right tool and when it is not.

The mathematics is the primary subject. Code appears only in service of it — to generate
figures and to show things the eye cannot derive by hand.

## How it's organized

The library is organized into **tracks**: ordered sequences where each article builds on
the one before it, so that *inch wide, mile deep* holds across a track as well as within a
single article. A track might be four articles or thirty; the subject sets the length.

These same source files produce two outputs. Each article renders on its own to a
self-contained PDF — grab one, print it, move on. The whole library also renders as this
single bound book, where **Parts are tracks and chapters are articles**, in track order,
with continuous numbering and cross-references resolved throughout.

Start anywhere a question interests you, or read a track front to back. Either way, the
intent is the same: leave with a question answered completely, not surveyed.
